text:
Royal fans have hailed Queen Mary of Denmark a "class act" for remembering her Aussie roots as she made a special visit to meet athletes in green and gold at the Olympics Village.

Royal fans have praised Queen Mary for taking the time to greet Aussie athletes at the Olympics Village, as the major sporting event gets underway in Paris.

The Tasmanian-born Queen made a special trip to meet the Australian Olympic Team ahead of the Opening Ceremony along the River Seine in Paris.

Photos shared on social media showed the mother-of-four smiling she took happy snaps with Aussie athletes on Friday, local time.

"Royalty in the Village! Australian-born Queen Mary of Denmark joined athletes in the Olympic Village," the Australian Olympic Team wrote on Instagram.

Denmark's Queen also stopped to have a chat with retired gold-medal Olympic track cyclist Anna Meares, who admitted she was a "bit nervous" to brush shoulders with the royal.

"Once an Aussie always an Aussie... and the Danish Queen," Meares wrote on Instagram.

"Great to see Queen Mary in the food court for lunch today.

"I was a bit nervous but she wished our athletes well and I did invite her to our allotment for an Aussie coffee."

Royal fans were quick to praise Denmark's newly-crowned Queen for remembering her Aussie roots.

"You can take the girl out of the country… but she bleeds green and gold!" one person declared.

"What a class act!" another said.

"Wonderful to see Queen Mary supporting the Australian athletes," a third chimed in.

"Aussie at heart," another said.

"Tassie Tassie Tassie Oi Oi Oi," a fifth joked.

"She may be Queen of Denmark, but she’ll always be an Aussie," another commented.

Other recalled the Sydney Olympics in 2000 whereMary first met her husband King of Denmark Frederik X- a love story which has captured the hearts of Aussie and Danish royal fans for more than two decades.

"To think it all started for her at an Olympics too!" one person remembered.

"Mary understands the magic that can happen at the Olympics," a second added.

Queen Mary and King Frederik are expected to attend various events in Paris over the coming days during their official visit for the Olympic Games.

The couple on Thursday cut the ribbon at the opening of the Danish Pavilion along Champs-Elysees, which will showcase Denmark's culture for those visiting Paris.

Mary stunned in bold red jumpsuit with a matching belt, while Frederik donned a navy suit and striped tie as they each cut the ribbon to the pavilion.

More than 460 athletes make up the Australian Olympic Team this year, as part of a broader 10,500 competitors from countries around the world for the 17-day event in Paris.
